steps.title: Understand that the solution is the intersection point
steps.content: For a system of linear equations, the solution is the point $(x, y)$ where the two lines intersect on the graph. This point satisfies both equations simultaneously.

steps.title: Locate the intersection on the graph
steps.content: Trace the two lines on the coordinate plane to find where they cross. One line is horizontal at $y = 3$, and the other is diagonal with positive slope. Following these lines to their meeting point shows they intersect at a specific coordinate pair.

steps.title: Read the coordinates of the intersection
steps.content: At the intersection point, read the $x$-coordinate by looking directly down to the $x$-axis, and read the $y$-coordinate by looking directly left to the $y$-axis. The two lines meet at $x = 4$ and $y = 3$, giving the point $(4, 3)$. That matches choice B — B is correct.
